title: Little Oddities Running Commentary of NGE Spoilers
display_title: Little Oddities Running Commentary of NGE Spoilers
wikitext:
[]: Little Oddities: Running Commentary of NGE (Spoilers)             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Fri Apr 09, 2010 7:55 pm I'm reviewing the entire series, and so far (at episode 6) I've already come up with some questions that aren't really worth individual threads, so this is a sort of weird little things thread. 1. Just what was the SSDF doing with a gigantic Eva sized positron rifle? What did they ever think they would need such a thing for? Maybe for Jet Alone? 2. Why did NERV make the pilot dressing area with the ability to see the naked silhouette of the pilot across the room from the pilot changing? 3. GNR + Shamshell. Sexy? I'll keep this updated as I go. EDIT 1: 4. Just what do they do with Angel bodies? Drill angel, whose name escapes me at the moment left that big drill just dangling, and it's huge-ass body lying in the middle of the city. It seems like disposing of the bodies would be rather problematic. By episode 6 they already have Shamshell and drill boy to deal with Sachiel was at least decent enough to save them the trouble. In rebuild at least they dissolve, but in the show they just lie around. What must that smell like? 5. Misato starts the morning off with an ice cold 9godawful) Yebisu. Yebisu is not a very good beer. People say American lagers are bad, which is true, but Japanese beer from my experience isn't very good. But is Misato buzzed at work all day or not? I suspect her of hitting up a flask when she tags along with Ritsuko for her smoke breaks. I guess this makes sense, as people used to drink on the job back before Carter was President, and they got shit done, but it seems maybe slightly irresponsible. NERV Intel has almost certainly got her entire house bugged and monitored, so her booze hounding wouldn't be an unknown. Is this bad, or awesome? 6. Why is Jet Alone? Why does it exist? Who thought that was a good idea?

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Fri Apr 09, 2010 8:14 pm [omnislash] 1. Just what was the SSDF doing with a gigantic Eva sized positron rifle? What did they ever think they would need such a thing for? Maybe for Jet Alone?

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Fri Apr 09, 2010 9:09 pm ran1 wrote: [omnislash] wat is this I don't even [/omnislash]

[]:             Quote Post by Octahedron » Sat Apr 10, 2010 2:02 am In the Rebuild atleast they state that the Positron Cannon is actually sopposed to be mobile artillery piece, they just hadn't finished it so they modified it into an Eva-sized sniper rifle. Jet Alone does seem like an utterly crazy idea tho. We've already established that Angels are pretty much immune to conventional weaponry. Evas can beat them because they can counter the ATF. Jet Alone can't. So if you can't kill it with nuke-equivalents, do you really think punching it with a giant robot would work? A giant, nuclear-powered robot, at that. The moment it gets damaged by an Angel, it's goign to start venting radioactive steam everywhere, or those stupid cooling rods snap off and it suffers a meltdown.

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Sat Apr 10, 2010 2:42 am I really don't think you can say enough bad shit about Jet Alone, but I finished up episode 16 and it's about bed time. I'll have more on Sunday.

[]: Re: Little Oddities: Running Commentary of NGE (Spoilers)             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Sat Apr 10, 2010 2:06 pm SenorSquiid wrote: 2. Why did NERV make the pilot dressing area with the ability to see the naked silhouette of the pilot across the room from the pilot changing?

[]:             Quote Post by ran1 » Sat Apr 10, 2010 2:11 pm Finally, I just don't understand why people don;t like both episode #07 and Jet Alone.

[]: Re: Little Oddities: Running Commentary of NGE (Spoilers)             Quote Post by Merridian » Sat Apr 10, 2010 2:27 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Finally, I just don't understand why people don;t like both episode #07 and Jet Alone. But we'll save that for the commentary.

[]: Re: Little Oddities: Running Commentary of NGE (Spoilers)             Quote Post by Reichu » Sat Apr 10, 2010 4:04 pm SenorSquiid wrote: GNR + Shamshell. Sexy?

[]: Re: Little Oddities: Running Commentary of NGE (Spoilers)             Quote Post by Joseph the PRPD » Sat Apr 10, 2010 4:05 pm Reichu wrote: Sure they dissolve -- but what do you do with all the "blood"?

[]: Re: Little Oddities: Running Commentary of NGE (Spoilers)             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Sat Apr 10, 2010 5:31 pm Reichu wrote: Sure they dissolve -- but what do you do with all the "blood"?

[]:             Quote Post by SenorSquiid » Sun Apr 11, 2010 7:53 pm I'm back from visiting mys sister, sos starting with episode 17. I know I skipped a few, but nothing really popped out at me between Magma Diver and Episode 16. good episodes, just nothign for this thread that caught my mind: -I really don't care for episode 17 much, but it serves it's purpose. It's sort of necessary filler, but it does an excellent job showing off Anno's directorial techniques and visual answers to verbal questions among other things. It's just not that good for re-watching. Nothing against it really, I like it more than I used to, but it's still not especially thrilling once you know Toji is going to be the pilot, and even when i first saw it I figured it out pretty fast. - Is it normal or socially acceptable in Japan to go into someone's apartment while they're gone and clean it? Rei doesn't really mind, but Rei's pretty laid back about privacy and intrusion and accidental groping. Seem's kind of weird on Shinji's part being all "She's not home. Well, let's go in and clean anyways." -Kaji is kind of a sexual predator the way he treats Misato, and in episode 17 poor Maya. At least Misato digs that sort of thing. -Speaking of Kaji, I've always enjoyed the relationship between him and Shinji. I wish it had been developed a little more like in the manga, but them's the brakes I suppose.

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Mon Apr 12, 2010 11:23 am 16. Does anyone know what the thing on pen Pen's back is? I've always wondered.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Mon Apr 12, 2010 12:13 pm SenorSquiid wrote: 10. The ending of episode 8 is red for some reason.

[]:             Quote Post by Lui » Thu Apr 15, 2010 8:45 pm Octahedron wrote: Jet Alone does seem like an utterly crazy idea tho. We've already established that Angels are pretty much immune to conventional weaponry. Evas can beat them because they can counter the ATF. Jet Alone can't.

[]:             Quote Post by skikes » Thu Apr 15, 2010 10:14 pm Octahedron wrote: Jet Alone does seem like an utterly crazy idea tho. We've already established that Angels are pretty much immune to conventional weaponry. Evas can beat them because they can counter the ATF. Jet Alone can't. So if you can't kill it with nuke-equivalents, do you really think punching it with a giant robot would work? A giant, nuclear-powered robot, at that. The moment it gets damaged by an Angel, it's goign to start venting radioactive steam everywhere, or those stupid cooling rods snap off and it suffers a meltdown.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Thu Apr 15, 2010 10:38 pm Power pack for his experimental symbiotic cyberbrain. Also has a fold-out drink tray.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 15, 2010 11:09 pm Lui wrote: I also wonder what happened to all the blood from angels..

[]:             Quote Post by Sachi » Thu Apr 15, 2010 11:17 pm Lui wrote: Then what was the point in disabling it? Wouldn't it have been easier and more effective to just allow it to be completely pwned by an angel rather than waste time hacking it?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Apr 15, 2010 11:38 pm The explanation I've heard is that Nerv hacked JA less out of humanitarian concerns and more out of an interest in preventing further resources from being diverted to Japan Heavy Industries. Make their lousy would-be Eva substitute look bad early on, keep it from eating up funds that Nerv needs for its own endeavors. Seele are always whining about money and Gendo has to spend lots of time traveling around trying to secure more of it, so hacking into Tokita's robot for the sake of yen doesn't seem too off. Misato "saving the day" wasn't planned, but, hey, so long as it all works out...

 it I figured it out pretty fast. - Is it normal or socially acceptable in Japan to go into someone's apartment while they're gone and clean it? Rei doesn't really mind, but Rei's pretty laid back about privacy and intrusion and accidental groping. Seem's kind of weird on Shinji's part being all "She's not home. Well, let's go in and clean anyways." -Kaji is kind of a sexual predator the way he treats Misato, and in episode 17 poor Maya. At least Misato digs that sort of thing. -Speaking of Kaji, I've always enjoyed the relationship between him and Shinji. I wish it had been developed a little more like in the manga, but them's the brakes I suppose.
